methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $37M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
44.01 is a Series A climate and energy company that has raised $37M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 3 same-stage, same-sector private companies (Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy) with valuations ranging from $110M to $200M, plus a broader comparable universe of 9 private firms and 1 public equity (LNZA).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy for post-money valuation and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression, yields $74M. Peer-median analysis across the three directly comparable companies produces $150M, with a range of $110M–$200M. These methods triangulate to a median valuation of $112M, with a defensible range of $63M (low) to $172M (high).

The $112M median represents a 3.0x step-up from raised capital and aligns closely with the lower quartile of the peer set, reflecting 44.01's earlier stage relative to some comparables. Key valuation uncertainties include technology maturity and path to revenue generation relative to peer firms, as well as potential differences in capital efficiency and market traction not visible in valuation alone.
